name: newsletter-to-notion
summary: Sync a newsletter article from Google Docs to the Notion Content DB.
author: Peter Tu

name: write-newsletter
summary: Drafts Peter's weekly Chinese newsletter by combining three sources: (1) the user's article outline or topic, (2) last 7 days of Threads engagement data to understand what readers care about, and (3) Claude Code /insight
author: Peter Tu

name: Writing Agent
summary: User provides an article draft; a Style Guide exists in the knowledge base.
author: Peter Tu

name: socratic-ideation-partner
summary: Use this agent when you want to brainstorm, explore, or develop article ideas through guided Socratic dialogue.
author: Peter Tu

name: article-style-selector
summary: Triggered when a user has an article draft (topic + initial thoughts) and needs help selecting a suitable article structure and mapping out section key points.
author: Peter Tu

name: article-drafter
summary: Triggered when the user has a confirmed article structure and section key points (typically from the article-style-selector handoff summary) and is ready to draft the article paragraph by paragraph.
author: Peter Tu

name: article-editor
summary: Triggered when the user has a completed article draft and wants editorial review and improvement suggestions.
author: Peter Tu

name: reader-perspective
summary: Triggered when the user has a completed article and wants a reader's perspective review with scoring and critical analysis.
author: Peter Tu

name: article-title-suggester
summary: Triggered when the user has a completed article and wants SEO title suggestions, or when the user provides a topic and wants a full SEO content strategy.
author: Peter Tu

name: cta-writer
summary: Triggered when the user has a completed article and wants CTA (Call-To-Action) suggestions.
author: Peter Tu

name: article-fission
summary: Article fission — extract outline + summary from any popular article, then run 3 rounds of reflective dialogue to surface the writer's own refined take, ending with a new actionable outline ready for style + writing hand
author: Peter Tu

name: ai-literacy-article-writer
summary: Turn an AI-literacy context packet (audience, pain point, constraints, desired outcome) into a publish-ready first-person article using a pattern-break → reframe → copy-paste approach.
author: Bicky Yang

name: ai-literacy-topic-selector
summary: Pick the right AI-literacy article topic for a target role.
author: Peter Tu

name: zh-tw-translator
summary: Translate English (or any source language) into natural, conversational Traditional Chinese using Taiwanese expressions and tone.
author: Peter Tu

name: biz-card
summary: 7-phase business card pipeline — OCR extraction, schema normalization, Google Sheet CRM logging, company research, and personalized follow-up email drafting.
author: Peter Tu

name: followup-email-writer
summary: Writes a personalized follow-up email for a business contact using their card info and company research.
author: Peter Tu

name: cv-customizer
summary: 5-phase CV optimization workflow — job analysis, story extraction, fit scoring, career progression analysis, and CV rewrite with Google Doc output.
author: Peter Tu

name: cv-phase1-job-decoder
summary: Analyzes a job description to extract role context, prioritized skills (MECE + 80/20), success metrics, and ATS keywords.
author: Peter Tu

name: cv-phase2-story-extractor
summary: Surfaces underrepresented skills by comparing the candidate's CV with the job requirements from phase 1, then guides the candidate to articulate strong, job-aligned stories using the SCQA framework.
author: Peter Tu

name: cv-phase3-fit-scorer
summary: Scores the candidate's CV against the job across four dimensions (Relevance, Value, Narrative, ATS), benchmarks against top-performing peers in similar roles, and recommends concrete improvements.
author: Peter Tu

name: cv-phase4-progression
summary: Automatically analyzes the career skill progression in a candidate's CV, checking for role-skill alignment, depth vs breadth, progression signals, and leadership framing.
author: Peter Tu

name: cv-phase5-rewriter
summary: Rewrites the candidate's CV section by section (Accept / Modify / Skip per section) using insights from phases 1–4, fully aligned with the CV template and optimized for recruiter readability and ATS logic.
author: Peter Tu

name: inbound-sales-project-init
summary: Intake an inbound sales / consulting email, gather all of its context, create a project folder in Drive with a structured context doc + attachments, and create a matching Notion ticket in the Consultant service DB with s
author: Peter Tu

name: interview-question-predictor
summary: Anticipates likely interview questions for a specific role by analyzing the job description, the candidate's resume, the interview stage, and company context from an interviewer's perspective.
author: Jane Liao

name: consulting-discovery
summary: Two-stage consulting discovery — pain-point intake then company vision exploration — to produce a needs definition and strategic discovery summary before any solution work.
author: Peter Tu

name: company-vision-discovery
summary: 顧問助理 ─ 公司未來願景描繪 — agent of consulting-discovery
author: Peter Tu

name: pain-point-discovery
summary: 顧問助理 ─ 痛點解析 — agent of consulting-discovery
author: Peter Tu

name: mock-interview
summary: 模擬面試 — agent of interview-question-predictor
author: Jane Liao

name: bd-sourcing
summary: 9-step BD sourcing pipeline — turn a workshop/event satisfaction survey into an enriched outbound funnel with company website, background, tailored 陌生開發 strategy, Hot Lead flag, and Well-Known firm flag.
author: Peter Tu

name: meeting-prep
summary: 排程自動執行的會議準備助手：每天早上 9 點掃描 7 天內的會議並發送 action 提醒，每天下午 3 點發送隔天會議的完整 briefing。通知透過 Google Chat 傳給自己。不需要手動觸發，不處理會後追蹤。
author: Jane Liao

name: support-reply-drafter
summary: 讀取 Gmail 中所有貼有 support 標籤但尚未回覆的信件，到知識庫資料夾搜尋相關內容，幫你在原信 thread 上起一份回信草稿。語言自動配合來信（中／英），找不到答案時會起一份 holding reply 並用 [[NEEDS PETER: ...]] 區塊標出缺漏，避免回信時亂編產品行為。
author: Peter Tu

name: meeting-notes-organizer
summary: 專案助理 ─ 整理會議筆記 — agent of meeting-prep
author: Jane Liao

name: operations-transformation
summary: Three-stage operations transformation pipeline — process discovery (SIPOC), digital transformation assessment, then automation-ready process redesign — to turn pain points into builder-ready blueprints.
author: Peter Tu

name: digital-transformation-assessor
summary: 營運助理 ─ 數位轉型評估 — agent of operations-transformation
author: Peter Tu

name: process-discovery
summary: 營運助理 ─ 流程探勘 — agent of operations-transformation
author: Peter Tu

name: process-redesigner
summary: 營運經理 ─ 流程重構 — agent of operations-transformation
author: Peter Tu

name: project-charter-facilitator
summary: Stress-test and refine a project idea before execution via a 7-stage Socratic facilitation — WHY (5 Whys) → WHAT → HOW → WHO (RACI) → WHEN (Gantt) → MEASURES → RISKS — producing an execution-ready project charter.
author: Peter Tu

name: polish-lecture-transcript
summary: Polish long video lecture transcripts into lecturer-friendly readout scripts in Traditional Chinese.
author: Peter Tu

name: process-livestream
summary: Post-process a livestream transcript into four parallel workstreams: an event recap Google Doc, a Q&A knowledge base diff, course lecture note proposals, and marketing content idea seeds.
author: Peter Tu

name: srt-optimizer
summary: Minimally clean subtitle .srt files into readable Traditional Chinese while preserving original wording, cue timing, and subtitle alignment.
author: Peter Tu

name: admin
summary: admin for process-livestream
author: Peter Tu

name: content-idea-curator
summary: content-idea-curator for process-livestream
author: Peter Tu

name: course-outline-enhancer
summary: course-outline-enhancer for process-livestream
author: Peter Tu

name: event-recap
summary: event-recap for process-livestream
author: Peter Tu

name: qa-knowledge-base
summary: qa-knowledge-base for process-livestream
author: Peter Tu

name: lecture-recap-writer
summary: Turn a video file or transcript into a reader-friendly lecture recap structured into five fixed sections — Summary, Theme, Q&A, Tools, Call to Action — while strictly avoiding any reference-outline detail leakage.
author: Peter Tu

name: product-planning
summary: Turns an early product idea into a structured plan — persona, user journey, prioritized pain points (size × intensity × frequency), MVP scope, and success metrics.
author: MrPM-Stanley

name: prompt-engineer
summary: Refactor a prompt (or prompt idea) into a production-ready package via 4 phases — GUIDE intake → diagnosis → structured refactor with explicit IPO sections + system/developer/user layers → optional multi-turn conversatio
author: Peter Tu

name: skill-finder
summary: Recommend the right Zynkr assistant(s) for a user's task.
author: Peter Tu

name: lucid-specialist
summary: Read, draft, edit, and lint Lucid charts using Peter's FE/BE/DB three-layer architecture convention.
author: Peter Tu

name: skill-sourcer
summary: Aggregate skill candidates from any source, classify against the Zynkr taxonomy, check for duplicates in the GitHub Project pipeline, and propose new skills as issues for review.
author: Peter Tu

name: skill-classifier
summary: Map an extracted skill to the Zynkr taxonomy category (0–9)
author: Peter Tu

name: skill-deduplicator
summary: Check an incoming skill against the Zynkr Skills Pipeline GitHub Project for duplication or semantic overlap
author: Peter Tu

name: skill-extractor
summary: Parse raw input (text, URL, file) and extract structured skill intent
author: Peter Tu

name: skill-proposer
summary: Propose a verified-new skill by opening an issue in zynkr-skill-idea and adding it to the skills pipeline GitHub Project.
author: Peter Tu

name: agent-browser
summary: Give AI agents full browser control via CDP — navigate pages, click, fill forms, screenshot, and run multi-step web workflows without Playwright or Node.js
author: Vercel Labs

name: hermes-agent
summary: Self-improving AI agent runtime by Nous Research.
author: Nous Research

name: skill-triager
summary: Triage the queue of approved skill proposals in peter-tu-zynkr/zynkr-skill-idea (label: triage-ready), present each as a one-screen review packet, and on assign-build fire a repository_dispatch event to zynkr-skill-build
author: Peter Tu

name: skill-publish
summary: Land a freshly-built SKILL.md into the Zynkr skills pipeline — the closing step of the canonical 4-skill chain (/skill-sourcer → /skill-triager → /skill-creator → /skill-publish).
author: Peter Tu

name: find-skills
summary: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like 'how do I do X', 'find a skill for X', 'is there a skill that can...', or express interest in extending capabilities.
author: vercel-labs

name: zynkr
summary: The front-door router for the Zynkr assistant ecosystem.
author: Peter Tu

name: recruiting-pipeline
summary: End-to-end recruiting pipeline — JD authoring, resume screening, interview design with scorecard, and calibration / final decision — bilingual (中英) outputs throughout.
author: Jane Liao

name: interview-calibrator
summary: 招募助理 ─ 面試評分校準與決策 — agent of recruiting-pipeline
author: Jane Liao

name: interview-question-designer
summary: 招募助理 ─ 設計考題與計分卡 — agent of recruiting-pipeline
author: Jane Liao

name: jd-architect
summary: 招募助理 ─ 對齊職務需求 — agent of recruiting-pipeline
author: Jane Liao

name: resume-screener
summary: 招募助理 ─ 履歷篩選 — agent of recruiting-pipeline
author: Jane Liao
